look.text: A rack of jackets, each more confident than the last. Yours came from here. Yours has since lost that confidence.

use.text: You browse for a replacement out of habit, then remember you have three dollars and a tab. You are not, currently, a browsing man.

itemUse.text: You hold the broken umbrella hopefully against the rack. It does not belong here. It does not belong anywhere. That's its whole thing.

look.text: RETURNS ACCEPTED — WITH RECEIPT, WITH TAGS, WITH A GOOD ATTITUDE, AND WITHIN A WINDOW WE WILL DEFINE AFTER YOU'VE WAITED. The attitude clause is hand-added. In pen. Recently.
A returns clerk who has weaponized the word 'policy.' She is not unkind. She is simply the last honest obstacle between you and four dollars.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: She examines the jacket, then the receipt, then the jacket. "...Tags on. Receipt valid. Attitude — acceptable." The register opens like a portcullis. FOUR DOLLARS. You are, briefly, solvent. She stamps the receipt VOID and hands it back — a souvenir of commerce survived.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "No receipt." She says it the way a judge says 'guilty.' "Bring the receipt or wear the jacket. Those are the two lives available to you."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "A receipt with no jacket is just... paper feelings. Bring me the item, sir."

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: "You may NOT click that at my register. This is a place of business and that click is a place of chaos."

exits.name: the exit
exits.look: Back to the block, hopefully four dollars richer.
